input.content:
No changes required — all AC items are already satisfied in the current code.

Verification (against cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/IntegrityPanel.svelte at base 0bccaf7c):

AC item 1: badge-status-blocked removed from non-status elements
  - grep for `badge-status-blocked` in IntegrityPanel.svelte: zero matches.

AC item 2: Issue ID chip uses neutral style (bg-bg-canvas border-border-line text-fg-muted)
  - Line 274: `class="rounded-none border border-border-line bg-bg-canvas px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] uppercase text-fg-muted dark:border-dark-border-line dark:bg-dark-bg-canvas dark:text-dark-fg-muted"` — matches exactly.

AC item 3: Action buttons use accent/semantic button patterns
  - Line 295 (Copy suggested unique ID button): uses `border-accent-lever bg-accent-lever/10 text-accent-lever` with dark variants.
  - Line 315 (Apply fix button): uses `border-accent-lever bg-accent-lever/10 text-accent-lever` with dark variants.

AC item 4: Diff text uses appropriate semantic colors with dark variants
  - Line 331: `text-status-blocked` (before)
  - Line 333: `text-status-closed` (after)
  - These classes are defined in src/app.css and resolve to `var(--status-blocked-text)` and `var(--status-closed-text)`. The CSS variables themselves are redefined under the `.dark` scope (app.css lines 96–110), so dark-mode adaptation is automatic without `dark:` utility prefixes. The previously cited `text-status-merged` token is no longer present (replaced with `text-status-closed`).

AC item 5: Error text uses text-error / dark:text-dark-error
  - Line 339: `class="text-xs text-error dark:text-dark-error"` — matches exactly.

AC item 6: Panel header uses text-headline-md font-headline-md
  - Line 215: `class="border-b border-border-line px-4 py-2 text-headline-md font-headline-md dark:border-dark-border-line"` — matches exactly.

Provenance: the substantive change set was committed as 4f347a98 ("fix(ui): use semantic tokens for IntegrityPanel non-status UI [ddx-7b4a39f5]") and is already present in the base revision 0bccaf7c of this execution worktree. The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note for this bead inspected a different attempt's diff that lacked IntegrityPanel.svelte changes — but the changes themselves were already merged earlier.

Blocking: nothing. No follow-up work needed; the bead can be closed. If the review keeps reopening because it inspects only the latest commit's diff (which here would be empty), the review heuristic should be widened to consider the file's current contents against the AC, not just the delta from the most recent attempt.

data.text: All six acceptance criteria are already satisfied in the current `IntegrityPanel.svelte` — the fix landed in commit `4f347a98` which is in the base of this worktree. No code changes needed; wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting per-AC verification with line numbers.
